description: Feature Showcase Pages - Build 9 individual SEO-optimized feature detail pages using a reusable template, covering calendar, jobs, fleet, bookings, comms, invoicing, dashboard, mobile app, and team chat

userStories.title: Feature page template component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable FeaturePage template so that all feature pages have consistent structure and can be built quickly from data.
acceptanceCriteria: Reusable FeaturePage component accepting typed FeaturePageData props

FeaturePageData type includes: title, tagline, description, heroImage, benefits array (icon + title + description), detailSections array (alternating image/text), relatedFeatures array (2-3 slugs), seo metadata (title, description, ogImage)

Template renders sections: 1. Hero (title+tagline+description+screenshot), 2. Key benefits (3-4 cards with Lucide icons), 3. Feature details (alternating left/right image+text sections), 4. Integration callout (how this feature connects to other VanMan features), 5. CTA section ('Start Free Trial' + 'See all features' link)
Breadcrumb component: Home > Features > [Feature Name]
Next feature / Previous feature navigation links at bottom (derived from features list order)
Related features section: 2-3 cards linking to other feature pages rendered from relatedFeatures array
All screenshots use Next.js Image component with responsive sizing and lightbox on click (view full-size)
All pages statically generated at build time
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This template is reused by all 9 feature pages below. Design: alternating text-left/image-right then text-right/image-left for visual variety.

userStories.title: Feature page dynamic route and data architecture
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a /features/[slug] route that generates static pages from feature data configs.
acceptanceCriteria: Dynamic route at app/features/[slug]/page.tsx
generateStaticParams exports all 9 feature slugs for static generation at build time
generateMetadata returns unique SEO title, meta description (compelling, under 160 chars, includes target keyword), and Open Graph image per feature
Page reads feature data from a config file (lib/feature-pages/[slug].ts) and passes to FeaturePage template
Returns 404 for unknown slugs via notFound()
Each feature config file exports typed FeaturePageData including seo metadata
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Feature slugs come from lib/features.ts created in PW-02. Feature page data configs are separate per-feature files with richer content.

userStories.title: Calendar & Scheduling feature page data
userStories.description: As a moving company owner, I want to understand VanMan's calendar so that I can see if it handles my scheduling needs.
acceptanceCriteria: Feature data config for calendar-scheduling slug in lib/feature-pages/calendar-scheduling.ts
Highlights: weekly time-grid view, drag-and-drop rescheduling, truck color-coding, real-time multi-user updates, mobile day view
Benefits: 'See your whole week at a glance', 'Reschedule in seconds', 'Multiple operators, one calendar'
Detail sections with screenshot placeholders for: calendar with color-coded jobs, drag interaction, mobile day view
Related features configured: job-management, fleet-management, mobile-app
SEO title: 'Moving Company Scheduling Software | VanMan'
SEO description targeting 'moving company scheduling software' keyword
Page renders correctly at /features/calendar-scheduling
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Screenshots should show realistic demo data from VanMan NZ, not lorem ipsum.

Detail sections with screenshot placeholders for: job detail page, locations tab with map, search results

userStories.title: Mobile App feature page data (iOS & Android)
userStories.description: As a moving company owner, I want to understand the mobile app so that I know my entire team can use VanMan in the field.
acceptanceCriteria: Feature data config for mobile-app slug in lib/feature-pages/mobile-app.ts
Highlights for Drivers: today's assigned jobs, tap-to-call customers, tap-to-navigate via Google Maps, start/finish delivery time tracking with background timer, add delivery notes

Highlights for Operators: create and edit jobs on the go (3-step wizard: General → Delivery → Tally), process payments (cash, POS, credit), send invoices via SMS/email, view full job calendar with filters
Highlights for Admins: full access to all mobile features plus staff management
Role-based views — drivers see simplified interface, operators see full controls
Works offline — cached data syncs when reconnected
Multi-tenant support — switch between business accounts
Benefits: 'Your whole team in one app', 'Drivers track time, operators manage jobs — all from their phone', 'Works even with patchy signal'
App Store and Google Play badge placeholders
Detail sections with screenshot placeholders for: driver job list, operator job creation wizard, time tracking screen, Google Maps navigation, tally/invoicing screen
Related features configured: calendar-scheduling, job-management, team-chat

SEO description targeting 'mobile app moving companies iOS Android' keyword
Page renders correctly at /features/mobile-app

userStories.notes: This page has the most content due to the multi-role nature of the app. Emphasize screenshots from all three roles.
